About: It is about the Virgin of Tenderness icon.
It is about the bond between mother and child.
Affects: It affects paintings from the following century.
It affects Florentine sculptures from the 1440–1450s.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Filippo Lippi's 1447 Enthroned Madonna and Child.
It is associated with Saint Luke, patron saint of artists.
It is associated with the Feast of the Pheasant.
It is associated with the Fall of Constantinople.
It is associated with the Bishop of Cambrai, John of Burgundy.
It is associated with John, Count of Étampes.
It is associated with Bonne of Artois.
It is associated with Simon Bening.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the Italo-Byzantine tradition.
It belongs to the Italian Quattrocento.
ConsistsOf: It consists of tempera on a cedar panel.
It consists of a gilded, decorated background.
It consists of Mary dressed in a blue robe with gold lined edging.
It consists of Christ with the body of an adult male.
It consists of a bent leg and another extended towards her.
It consists of a right arm held upright and intimately holding her chin from below.
CreatedTo: It was created to serve as a bridge between the Byzantine icon tradition and the Italian Quattrocento.
It was created to rally sufficient religious fervour to launch a crusade to retake the city.
HasA: It has a tender manner in which the child is nestled against her cheek.
It has a tilted head towards her son.
It has arms that warmly cradle him.
It has one leg bent and another extended towards her.
It has a right arm held upright and intimately holding her chin from below.
It has subtle modeling of the faces.
It has a volumetric aspect of the draperies with soft folds.
It has Latin inscriptions.
It has an elaborate punchwork of the haloes.
It has an unusually long forehead.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by the tender manner in which the child is nestled against her cheek.
It is characterized by the closeness and engagement of mother and child.
It is characterized by subtle modeling of the faces.
It is characterized by the volumetric aspect of the draperies with soft folds.
It is characterized by Latin inscriptions.
It is characterized by the style of the elaborate punchwork of the haloes.
It is characterized by the closeness of the two figures' faces.
It is characterized by their seeming embrace.
It is characterized by the unusual concentration of the child's facial features in a small portion of his head.
It is characterized by the Madonna's gaze being significantly altered in some adaptations.
HeldIn: It was held in the Old Cambrai Cathedral.
It was held in its Chapel of the Holy Trinity.
It was held in the Nelson-Atkins Museum of Art in Kansas City, Missouri.
It was held in the Museum of Fine Arts, Ghent.
HeldOn: It was held on the eve of the Feast of the Assumption.

Influenced: It influenced a wide number of paintings from the following century.
